title: [lichen_l2.c:580] last_seen timestamp updated even if constant-time loop continues
description:
Gap: In peer_try_all_pubkeys, last_seen is updated only for the FIRST matching peer if multiple peers could verify (duplicate keys).

What: Lines 556-559 record the first matching peer. Line 580 updates last_seen for that peer. If (hypothetically) two peers had the same public key registered, only the first one's last_seen would be updated, and the loop would continue, potentially recording a different found_idx.

Why it matters: While duplicate pubkeys are unlikely in practice (the same Ed25519 keypair registered under two EUI-64s), the code structure allows for this ambiguity. The 'if (found_idx < 0)' check on line 556 prevents updating found_idx after the first match, but the semantic is subtle. Could lead to LRU eviction of the wrong peer entry.
close_reason: By design: Duplicate pubkeys across different EUI-64 entries is architecturally impossible because the peer's EUI-64 IS derived from their pubkey (SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8]). The same pubkey always produces the same EUI-64, so there cannot be 'two peers with the same public key registered under two EUI-64s'. The found_idx logic correctly handles the only valid case: one pubkey = one peer entry.
